Why Choose Us

On-site training that works around your business

We come to you. No travel, no time off site for your team. Our trainers bring over two decades of teaching experience.

  • Training at your premises — minimal disruption to operations
  • FAIB accredited (Highfield regulated qualifications also available)
  • Flexible scheduling including evenings and weekends
  • Group rates — cost-effective for 4+ delegates
  • Covers all of Nottinghamshire and Derbyshire
  • Experienced trainers

Is on-site training right for you?

If you have 4 or more staff needing first aid training, on-site delivery is usually the most cost-effective and convenient solution. We bring all equipment.

Recommended minimum: 4 delegates for on-site delivery. Smaller groups can be accommodated — just get in touch to discuss.

First Aid Course

Emergency First Aid at Work

A one-day qualification covering the essential skills to manage first aid emergencies. The most widely held workplace first aid certificate — ideal for lower-risk environments.

Home›Courses›Emergency First Aid at Work

Who is this course for?

The Emergency First Aid at Work (EFAW) qualification is designed for any employee who may need to respond to a first aid emergency at work. It is particularly suited to:

  • Appointed first aiders in offices, retail, hospitality, and other lower-risk workplaces
  • Small businesses where a full FAW is not required by the risk assessment
  • Employees where the HSE needs assessment indicates EFAW is appropriate
  • Staff needing a refresh of emergency first aid skills

What does the course cover?

  • Role and responsibilities of the first aider
  • Managing an unconscious casualty and recovery position
  • Cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) — adult
  • Use of an Automated External Defibrillator (AED)
  • Choking — adults
  • Bleeding and shock — recognition and management
  • Burns and scalds
  • Seizures
  • Minor injuries

Assessment

Assessment is continuous and practical throughout the course. Delegates are assessed on practical skill demonstrations and an MCQ exam / questioning.

Accreditation

Accredited through the First Aid Industry Body (FAIB). A regulated qualification through Highfield Qualifications is also available on request.

FAIB AccreditedHighfield Regulated Available

Course at a glance

Duration6 hours (1 day)
AudienceLower-risk workplaces; smaller businesses
AssessmentContinuous practical & MCQ exam / questioning
CertificateValid for 3 years
DeliveryOn-site at your premises
AccreditationFAIB / Highfield (regulated)
AreaNottinghamshire & Derbyshire
First Aid Course

First Aid at Work

The comprehensive three-day workplace first aid qualification. Required in higher-risk environments and larger organisations — the gold standard for appointed first aiders.

Home›Courses›First Aid at Work

Who is this course for?

The First Aid at Work (FAW) qualification is the most comprehensive workplace certificate and is required in many environments. Suitable for:

  • Appointed first aiders in manufacturing, construction, engineering, warehousing and other higher-risk settings
  • Larger organisations requiring a more thorough level of first aid provision
  • Workplaces where the HSE risk assessment specifies FAW
  • Those seeking the most highly regarded workplace first aid qualification

What does the course cover?

The FAW course significantly expands on the EFAW, covering a much wider range of injuries and medical emergencies:

  • Role and responsibilities of the workplace first aider
  • Managing an unconscious casualty; CPR and AED
  • Choking — adults
  • Wounds, severe bleeding and shock
  • Burns and scalds
  • Bone, muscle and joint injuries — fractures, dislocations, sprains
  • Head, neck and spinal injuries
  • Chest injuries
  • Eye injuries
  • Sudden illness — heart attack, stroke, seizures, diabetes, asthma, anaphylaxis
  • Poisoning
  • First aid reporting and record keeping

Assessment

Continuous practical assessment across all three days plus an MCQ exam / questioning. Delegates must demonstrate competency in all required skills.

Accreditation

Accredited through FAIB. Regulated qualification through Highfield Qualifications also available.

FAIB AccreditedHighfield Regulated Available

Course at a glance

Duration18 hours (3 days)
AudienceHigher-risk workplaces; larger organisations
AssessmentContinuous practical & MCQ exam / questioning
CertificateValid for 3 years
DeliveryOn-site at your premises
AccreditationFAIB / Highfield (regulated)
AreaNottinghamshire & Derbyshire
First Aid Course

First Aid at Work Requalification

Renew your First Aid at Work certificate with the two-day requalification course. Certificates can be requalified up to 3 months after expiry — but until requalified, the holder cannot act as a workplace first aider.

Home›Courses›FAW Requalification

Who is this course for?

The FAW Requalification is for holders of a current, valid First Aid at Work certificate who need to renew before it expires. Suitable for:

  • Current FAW certificate holders with a certificate due to expire within 3 months
  • Appointed workplace first aiders maintaining their qualification
  • Organisations keeping their first aid team fully certified

Certificate expiry rules: Learners may take the requalification course up to 3 months after their certificate has expired. However, once a certificate has expired the holder cannot act as a workplace first aider until they have successfully requalified. If the certificate has been expired for more than 3 months, the learner must take the full three-day First Aid at Work course — the requalification is not available in this case.

What does the course cover?

The course revisits and refreshes all key FAW topics with an emphasis on practical skills, updated guidelines and scenario-based practice:

  • Refresher on the first aider's role and responsibilities
  • CPR, AED and unconscious casualty management
  • Wounds, bleeding and shock
  • Burns, scalds and eye injuries
  • Bone and joint injuries
  • Medical emergencies — heart attack, stroke, seizures, asthma, anaphylaxis, diabetes
  • Any updates to first aid guidelines or best practice

Assessment

Continuous practical assessment across both days plus an MCQ exam / questioning — consistent with the full FAW qualification standards.

Accreditation

Accredited through FAIB. Regulated qualification through Highfield Qualifications also available.

FAIB AccreditedHighfield Regulated Available

Course at a glance

Duration12 hours (2 days)
AudienceFAW certificate holders (requalification available up to 3 months after expiry)
AssessmentContinuous practical & MCQ exam / questioning
CertificateValid for 3 years
PrerequisiteFAW cert required (requalification available up to 3 months after expiry)
DeliveryOn-site at your premises
AccreditationFAIB / Highfield (regulated)
AreaNottinghamshire & Derbyshire
Paediatric First Aid

Emergency Paediatric First Aid

A one-day paediatric qualification for those working with children in lower-risk settings — childminders, teaching assistants and after-school staff.

Home›Courses›Emergency Paediatric First Aid

Who is this course for?

The Emergency Paediatric First Aid (EPFA) qualification is designed for anyone working with or caring for babies and children who requires a paediatric-focused first aid certificate:

  • Registered childminders (one PFA certificate required per Ofsted)
  • Teaching assistants, early years support staff and after-school workers
  • Nannies, au pairs and private childcare workers
  • Play workers and youth workers

Note: Ofsted-registered nurseries and day care providers may require the full two-day Paediatric First Aid (PFA) qualification. Check your registration requirements before booking.

What does the course cover?

  • Role of the paediatric first aider
  • Managing an unresponsive baby or child
  • CPR for infants and children
  • Choking in babies and children
  • Shock — recognition and management
  • Bleeding and wounds
  • Burns and scalds
  • Febrile seizures
  • Common childhood medical conditions

Assessment

Continuous practical assessment throughout the day, including paediatric CPR and choking scenarios, plus an MCQ exam / questioning.

Accreditation

Accredited through FAIB. Regulated qualification through Highfield Qualifications also available.

FAIB AccreditedHighfield Regulated Available

Course at a glance

Duration6 hours (1 day)
AudienceChildminders, TAs, childcare workers
AssessmentContinuous practical & MCQ exam / questioning
CertificateValid for 3 years
DeliveryOn-site at your premises
AccreditationFAIB / Highfield (regulated)
AreaNottinghamshire & Derbyshire
Paediatric First Aid

Paediatric First Aid

The full two-day paediatric qualification. Required by Ofsted-registered nurseries, day care and early years settings under the EYFS statutory framework.

Home›Courses›Paediatric First Aid

Who is this course for?

The Paediatric First Aid (PFA) is the full qualification required by Ofsted-registered early years settings under the EYFS statutory framework. Suitable for:

  • Nursery staff and room leaders
  • Day care and crèche workers
  • Out-of-school clubs, holiday clubs and forest schools
  • Primary school staff requiring the full paediatric qualification
  • Childminders wishing to hold the comprehensive certificate

Ofsted requirement: The EYFS framework requires at least one person with a current PFA certificate to be on the premises and available at all times children are present in registered settings.

What does the course cover?

All EPFA topics plus additional content across the second day:

  • All Emergency Paediatric First Aid content
  • Head injuries
  • Spinal injuries
  • Fractures and dislocations
  • Allergic reactions and anaphylaxis
  • Asthma in children
  • Diabetic emergencies
  • Meningitis — recognition and response
  • Safeguarding awareness for first aiders
  • Record keeping and incident reporting

Assessment

Continuous practical assessment across both days, infant and child CPR and scenario assessments, plus an MCQ exam / questioning.

Accreditation

Accredited through FAIB. Regulated qualification through Highfield Qualifications available — recommended for Ofsted-registered settings where a regulated certificate may be requested as evidence.

FAIB AccreditedHighfield Regulated Available

Course at a glance

Duration12 hours (2 days)
AudienceNurseries, EYFS settings, day care
AssessmentContinuous practical & MCQ exam / questioning
CertificateValid for 3 years
OfstedSatisfies EYFS PFA requirement
DeliveryOn-site at your premises
AccreditationFAIB / Highfield (regulated)
AreaNottinghamshire & Derbyshire
About Us

Home›About Us
Our Story

Expert training from a real industry professional

Notts & Derby First Aid is a trading name of MG Training Ltd, founded in 2005, specialising in compliance training from a base in Nottingham.

Our director has a background in hospitality operations but has been lecturing in further education since 2005, training a large number of learners across multiple compliance disciplines.

That depth of experience means our training goes far beyond ticking boxes — it's practical, relevant, and delivered by someone who understands the workplaces and industries they train.

MG Training

Based at NBV Enterprise Centre, Davids Lane, Old Basford, Nottingham. Specialist compliance training across multiple disciplines — delivered in-house and throughout the East Midlands.

Notts & Derby First Aid is our dedicated first aid brand, covering Nottinghamshire and Derbyshire with on-site workplace and paediatric first aid training.

2005
Teaching since

Subject areas
MG Training Course Areas

Other qualifications delivered by MG Training

In addition to first aid, MG Training delivers accredited and regulated qualifications across the following compliance disciplines.

Licensing

Level 2 Award for Personal Licence Holders (APLH) and licensing law training under the Licensing Act 2003.

Food Safety

Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 Food Safety & Hygiene qualifications for food handlers, supervisors, managers and senior food safety professionals in catering, hospitality and retail environments.

HACCP

Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points training for food manufacturers and catering operations — a legal requirement for food businesses and a cornerstone of effective food safety management systems.

Health & Safety

Practical workplace health and safety training including risk assessment, legal responsibilities and compliance for businesses across all sectors.

Education & Training

Teaching, training and assessment qualifications for trainers, assessors and Internal Quality Assurers (IQAs) working in further education and workplace learning.

Visit MG Training

For all other compliance training enquiries, visit mgtraining.co.uk

Help & Information

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about our courses, on-site delivery, accreditation and certificates.

Home›FAQs
Common Questions

Got a question? We've got answers.

Can't find what you need? Contact us directly and we'll be happy to help.

What is the difference between EFAW and FAW?▼
Emergency First Aid at Work (EFAW) is a one-day course covering core emergency skills, suitable for lower-risk workplaces. First Aid at Work (FAW) is a comprehensive three-day qualification covering a much wider range of injuries and illnesses, required in higher-risk environments. The appropriate course depends on your workplace risk assessment — we're happy to advise.
How do we decide which first aid course our business needs?▼
The HSE recommends completing a first aid needs assessment considering the number of employees, the nature of the work, workplace hazards and location. We can help you work through this — get in touch for free advice on which course is right for your business.
How long are first aid certificates valid?▼
All of our first aid certificates are valid for three years. We strongly recommend requalifying before expiry — if a certificate lapses, delegates must retake the full qualification rather than the shorter requalification course. We can send reminders ahead of renewal dates.
What areas do you cover for on-site training?▼
We deliver throughout Nottinghamshire and Derbyshire, including Nottingham, Derby, Mansfield, Newark, Chesterfield, Matlock, Long Eaton, Hucknall, Ilkeston and surrounding areas. If you're unsure whether we can reach your location, please get in touch.
What do we need to provide for on-site training?▼
We bring all training materials, CPR manikins and equipment. You need to provide a suitable room with enough floor space for practical activities, seating for all delegates, and ideally a table or desk area. Let us know your space and we'll confirm suitability.
What is the minimum number of delegates for on-site training?▼
We recommend a minimum of 4 delegates to make on-site delivery cost-effective. However, smaller groups can be accommodated — contact us for a tailored quote. Maximum group size is typically 12 to maintain training quality.
What is FAIB and are the qualifications recognised?▼
The First Aid Industry Body (FAIB) is the recognised awarding and accreditation body for first aid qualifications in the UK. FAIB-accredited certificates are widely accepted by employers and HSE-compliant. We also offer Highfield Qualifications (Ofqual-regulated) versions where a regulated certificate is required.
Are your qualifications accepted by Ofsted?▼
Yes. Our Paediatric First Aid qualification meets the EYFS statutory requirement. We recommend the Highfield-regulated version for Ofsted-registered settings — please mention this when booking so we issue the correct certificate.
Can training days be spread out rather than consecutive?▼
For multi-day courses we can discuss flexible scheduling. Days don't have to be consecutive but should be completed within a reasonable timeframe to maintain continuity. Contact us to arrange dates that suit your operation.
Does MG Training offer other compliance courses?▼
Yes — MG Training also delivers Licensing (APLH), Food Safety, HACCP, Health & Safety, and Education & Training qualifications. See our About Us page for details, or visit mgtraining.co.uk.

Flexible Study Options

Blended Learning First Aid

Complete the theory online at your own pace, then attend a shorter practical classroom session at your premises. Same qualification, smarter delivery.

Home›Blended Learning
How It Works

Learn online first, then practise in person

Blended learning splits the qualification into an online theory element and a shorter practical classroom session. The e-learning must be completed before the classroom day.

1

Complete e-learning

Work through online modules at your own pace. Access provided when you confirm your booking.

2

Finish before class

All e-learning must be completed before the classroom day. Delegates who have not finished cannot take part.

3

Classroom at your site

Shorter on-site practical day focused on skills, scenarios and assessment. Same standard, less time on site.

Course Breakdown

Available blended learning courses

Four of our five courses are available with a blended option. EPFA requires full classroom attendance only.

Blended Available

Emergency First Aid at Work

2 hrs e-learning4 hrs classroom6 hrs total

Complete 2 hours of online theory before attending a 4-hour practical classroom session at your premises. Knowledge and theory covered online, classroom time focused entirely on skills and assessment.

E-learning must be completed before the classroom session.

E-learning2 hours
Classroom4 hours
Total6 hours
Certificate3 years
Blended Available

First Aid at Work

6 hrs e-learning12 hrs classroom (2 days)18 hrs total

Complete 6 hours of online theory before attending two days (12 hours) of practical training at your premises. Replaces the traditional three-day course while maintaining the same qualification standard.

All e-learning must be completed before the classroom days. The two days can be consecutive or on separate dates.

E-learning6 hours
Classroom12 hours (2 days)
Total18 hours
Certificate3 years
Blended Available

First Aid at Work Requalification

6 hrs e-learning6 hrs classroom (1 day)12 hrs total

Requalify with 6 hours of online refresher learning followed by a single focused classroom day. Available to FAW certificate holders up to 3 months after expiry. Certificate holders whose certificate has expired cannot act as a workplace first aider until they requalify. Expired more than 3 months — the full FAW course is required.

E-learning must be completed before the classroom day.

Classroom6 hours (1 day)

Important

E-learning must be completed first

The online element is a mandatory part of the qualification. Delegates who arrive at the classroom session without completing the e-learning will be unable to participate and may lose their place.

E-learning access links and instructions are provided when you confirm your booking.

Same qualification. Smarter delivery.

Blended learning leads to exactly the same FAIB-accredited or Highfield-regulated certificate as the full classroom version. No difference in qualification, assessment standard or the certificate you receive.

Interested in blended learning for your team?

We will arrange e-learning access and schedule the classroom day at your premises.
